heading: Drug and alcohol abuse prevention
label: 1011i Drug and alcohol abuse prevention
notes: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to subsecs. (a) to (d) of this section were contained in prior to repeal by .section 1145g of this titlePub. L. 105–244

children.label: A
children.text: standards of conduct that clearly prohibit, at a minimum, the unlawful possession, use, or distribution of illicit drugs and alcohol by students and employees on the institution’s property or as part of any of the institution’s activities;

children.label: B
children.text: a description of the applicable legal sanctions under local, State, or Federal law for the unlawful possession or distribution of illicit drugs and alcohol;

children.label: C
children.text: a description of the health-risks associated with the use of illicit drugs and the abuse of alcohol;

children.label: D
children.text: a description of any drug or alcohol counseling, treatment, or rehabilitation or re-entry programs that are available to employees or students; and

children.label: E
children.text: a clear statement that the institution will impose sanctions on students and employees (consistent with local, State, and Federal law), and a description of those sanctions, up to and including expulsion or termination of employment and referral for prosecution, for violations of the standards of conduct required by subparagraph (A); and

children.label: A
children.text: determine the program’s effectiveness and implement changes to the program if the changes are needed;

children.label: i
children.text: occur on the institution’s campus (as defined in ), or as part of any of the institution’s activities; andsection 1092(f)(6) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: are reported to campus officials;

children.label: C
children.text: determine the number and type of sanctions described in paragraph (1)(E) that are imposed by the institution as a result of drug and alcohol-related violations and fatalities on the institution’s campus or as part of any of the institution’s activities; and

children.label: D
children.text: ensure that the sanctions required by paragraph (1)(E) are consistently enforced.

children.heading: Information availability
children.label: b Information availability
children.text: Each institution of higher education that provides the certification required by subsection (a) shall, upon request, make available to the Secretary and to the public a copy of each item required by subsection (a)(1) as well as the results of the biennial review required by subsection (a)(2).

children.label: A
children.text: the periodic review of a representative sample of programs required by subsection (a); and

children.label: B
children.text: a range of responses and sanctions for institutions of higher education that fail to implement their programs or to consistently enforce their sanctions, including information and technical assistance, the development of a compliance agreement, and the termination of any form of Federal financial assistance.

children.heading: Rehabilitation program
children.label: 2 Rehabilitation program
children.text: The sanctions required by subsection (a)(1)(E) may include the completion of an appropriate rehabilitation program.

children.heading: Appeals
children.label: d Appeals
children.text: Upon determination by the Secretary to terminate financial assistance to any institution of higher education under this section, the institution may file an appeal with an administrative law judge before the expiration of the 30-day period beginning on the date such institution is notified of the decision to terminate financial assistance under this section. Such judge shall hold a hearing with respect to such termination of assistance before the expiration of the 45-day period beginning on the date that such appeal is filed. Such judge may extend such 45-day period upon a motion by the institution concerned. The decision of the judge with respect to such termination shall be considered to be a final agency action.

children.heading: Alcohol and drug abuse prevention grants
children.label: e Alcohol and drug abuse prevention grants

children.heading: Program authority
children.label: 1 Program authority
children.text: The Secretary may make grants to institutions of higher education or consortia of such institutions, and enter into contracts with such institutions, consortia, and other organizations, to develop, implement, operate, improve, and disseminate programs of prevention, and education (including treatment-referral) to reduce and eliminate the illegal use of drugs and alcohol and the violence associated with such use. Such grants or contracts may also be used for the support of a higher education center for alcohol and drug abuse prevention that will provide training, technical assistance, evaluation, dissemination, and associated services and assistance to the higher education community as determined by the Secretary and institutions of higher education.

children.heading: Awards
children.label: 2 Awards
children.text: Grants and contracts shall be awarded under paragraph (1) on a competitive basis.

children.heading: Applications
children.label: 3 Applications
children.text: An institution of higher education, a consortium of such institutions, or another organization that desires to receive a grant or contract under paragraph (1) shall submit an application to the Secretary at such time, in such manner, and containing or accompanied by such information as the Secretary may reasonably require by regulation.

children.label: i
children.text: the equitable participation of private and public institutions of higher education (including community and junior colleges); and

children.label: ii
children.text: the equitable geographic participation of such institutions.

children.heading: Consideration
children.label: B Consideration
children.text: In awarding grants and contracts under this subsection the Secretary shall give appropriate consideration to institutions of higher education with limited enrollment.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 5 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection such sums as may be necessary for fiscal year 2009 and each of the five succeeding fiscal years.
